requirement.original: Fix 6 outstanding items from this session's Cycle 1-6 work: (1) BA spec_path transparency, (2) AC-02 INJECTION_PROOF runtime e2e, (3+4) Hook shell parsing ANSI-C bypass + over-blocking, (5) Glob entry-gate gap, (6) spec-20260518-225715 Cycle 4/5/6 append
requirement.clarified: Six accumulated fix-forward items from Cycles 1-6: one commands/dev.md orchestrator patch, one zero-code QA verification, one hook security fix (shlex rewrite of Layer 1.F), one entry-gate regex fix, and one spec data append.
requirement.what: Close 6 specific gaps identified during /close review of Cycles 1-6
requirement.why: Items 3+4+5 are security-adjacent (hook bypass/false-positive). Item 1 is downstream transparency. Item 2 is contract verification. Item 6 is data integrity.

included: commands/dev.md Step 5 validation — inject spec_path into context.json
hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh Layer 1.F — shlex-based token parsing for compound/write detection
hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh Layer 1.F — entry-gate regex glob metachar fix
QA report verification of INJECTION_PROOF 4 fields (zero code)

success_criteria: context.json carries spec_path when --spec is passed
ANSI-C quoting $'-fprint' is detected by Layer 1.F
grep -E 'foo|bar' against protected path is not false-positive blocked
constraints: Python stdlib shlex only (specifically shlex.shlex with posix=True and punctuation_chars=True, set commenters='' and whitespace_split=True via attribute assignment — NOT plain shlex.split, see iter-1 codex F3 and iter-2 codex C1) — no external dependencies
Fail-closed on tokenizer ValueError when list(lex) is exhausted (deny, not allow — see AC-08 and iter-2 codex C11)

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Six accumulated gaps from Cycle 1-6 /close reviews
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Items 3+4: Layer 1.F compound-shape detector and write-action veto use raw-text grep on serialized command strings, which cannot distinguish shell metacharacters inside quoted contexts from actual shell operators. Item 5: entry-gate regex character class excludes glob metacharacters. Item 1: BA agent spec has no spec_path field in its output schema. Item 6: manual data entry not yet performed.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: 867042b - fix(security): close Layer 1.F compound-command bypass (introduced the compound detector that over-blocks and does not handle ANSI-C)
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Commit 867042b added compound-shape detection using grep patterns against raw command text. This was a valid defense against compound bypasses but did not account for quoting semantics.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Raw grep cannot distinguish `|` as a shell pipe from `|` inside a quoted regex argument. Similarly, ANSI-C quoting like $'-fpr''int' splits the dangerous token across two shell words, defeating grep pattern matching.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Items 3+4+5: introduced by commit 867042b (2026-05-25). Items 1+6: accumulated gaps from Cycles 1-6 (2026-05-19 through 2026-05-25).
observations: ANSI-C bypass: printf '%s' "find /tmp $'-fpr''int' /tmp/out" | grep -qE '-fprint' returns rc=1 (observed: BA subprocess test)
Over-blocking: printf '%s' "grep -E 'foo|bar' somefile" | grep -qE '|' returns rc=0 false positive (observed: BA subprocess test)
Glob entry-gate: echo '/tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-*.flag' | grep entry-gate-regex returns rc=1 NO MATCH (observed: BA subprocess test)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: BA ran 3 subprocess tests against the current hook. All 3 confirmed the reported behaviors: ANSI-C bypass (rc=1 when should be caught), over-blocking (rc=0 false positive on quoted |), glob entry-gate miss (rc=1 on glob path).

git_bisect_result.commit_message: fix(security): close Layer 1.F compound-command bypass in pretool-bash-safety.sh (task 20260525-095242)

attempts.proposed_solution: Replace PREFIX-read allowlist with WHOLE-COMMAND-read predicate stack using 7 grep predicates
attempts.actual_change: Layer 1.F lines 633-758 rewritten with compound-shape detector using raw grep
attempts.outcome: Fixed compound bypass (;, &&, ||) but introduced ANSI-C bypass and over-blocking regression

novelty_check.rationale: Prior attempt used raw-text grep at L4; this attempt replaces grep with shlex.shlex(posix=True, punctuation_chars=True) tokenizer parsing at L4 (NOT plain shlex.split, which was the rejected approach per iter-1 codex F3). Different mechanism within same layer: tokenization vs pattern matching. The root cause class (quoting-unaware text matching) is addressed by the mechanism change.

recent_commits: aea75d9 chore(docs): ship cycle 20260522-000000 close-report and QA verification

development_approach.strategy: Item 1 (OBJ-1 + iter-2 C3): Add spec_path injection to commands/dev.md Step 5 validation -- overwrite when null/absent/empty/different (codex iter-1 F1). Negative-case wording uses 'orchestrator-resolved spec_path == null' (codex iter-2 C3). Items 3+4 (OBJ-6 unified): Replace raw-grep compound/write detectors with the canonical tokenizer recipe: `lex = shlex.shlex(cmd, posix=True, punctuation_chars=True); lex.commenters = ''; lex.whitespace_split = True; tokens = list(lex)` -- attribute assignment NOT constructor kwarg (codex iter-2 C1: shlex.shlex(..., commenters='') is invalid Python). Plain shlex.split is forbidden (iter-1 codex F3 showed unspaced separators are missed). Write-action veto must also match $-prefixed tokens for ANSI-C bypass (iter-1 codex F2: shlex yields $-fprint not -fprint) and must generalize across find write-action family (iter-2 C9: also -delete, -exec, etc., not special-case -fprint only). Quote-state-tracking caveat (iter-2 C6 + C8): single-quoted '$(cmd)' and unquoted $(cmd) tokenize identically under shlex; to distinguish AC-04 quoted-ALLOW from quoted/unquoted-active-DENY, the implementation must EITHER check tokens for retained leading $/<>/ chars OR run a raw-text inspection pass. Implementation can be inline Python in bash or a helper function -- dev decides (iter-1 codex F7). Item 5 (OBJ-5 + iter-2 C10): Switch entry-gate to substring match (grep -F) or validated regex -- do NOT use the invalid regex [A-Za-z0-9_-*?[\]]* (iter-1 codex F5). Bracket forms [abc] and [!abc] are POSIX shell-glob syntax (the ! is bracket-expression negation, not a 5th char) -- regex impl must match literal [, ], !, *, ?. Tokenizer error path (iter-2 C11): exhaust list(lex) in try/except ValueError: deny() -- construction alone does not raise (AC-08). Item 6: Append Cycle 4/5/6 sections with non-empty content from completion reports.
development_approach.validation_approach: Items 3+4+5: pytest tests invoking the hook with mocked payloads covering ANSI-C bypass, over-blocking false positive, and glob entry gate. Item 1: verify context.json output. Item 6: grep for Cycle 4/5/6 headers.

_risk_level_doc: medium: security-adjacent hook parsing fix affecting pretool-bash-safety.sh (4 files touched, includes safety hook).

research_findings.reason: Straightforward bug fixes with clear root causes in existing hook code; the chosen tokenizer (shlex.shlex with posix=True and punctuation_chars=True) is stdlib, well-understood. Iter-2 codex review of the AC matrices substitutes for further research.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: 7 findings, all with PROPOSED_FIX. Key issues: (F1) AC-01 patch must handle null not just absent, (F2) shlex does not expand ANSI-C $'...' so write-action veto needs $-prefix matching, (F3) plain shlex.split misses unspaced separators -- must use punctuation_chars=True, (F4) AC-05 non-discriminating -- added AC-05b, (F5) suggested glob regex is invalid grep syntax, (F6) AC-06 can pass with empty headers, (F7) scope ambiguity on helper file creation.

findings.issue: AC-01 patch condition too narrow (absent vs null)
findings.rationale: BA context.json already writes spec_path: null, so 'absent' check misses it

findings.issue: shlex.split does not expand ANSI-C $'...' to -fprint
findings.proposed_fix: Write-action veto must also match $-prefixed tokens

findings.issue: shlex.split misses unspaced separators (ls f;python3 -> [ls, f;python3])
findings.rationale: Regression risk for prior Cycle 6 compound bypass fix

findings.issue: AC-05 non-discriminating (rc=0 both before and after fix)
findings.proposed_fix: Add negative glob case with compound command

findings.issue: Suggested glob regex [A-Za-z0-9_-*?[\]]* is invalid grep syntax
findings.proposed_fix: Use substring match or validated regex
findings.rationale: grep: Invalid range end confirmed by codex test

findings.issue: AC-06 can pass with empty cycle headers
findings.proposed_fix: Require non-empty content under each header

findings.issue: Scope ambiguity on helper file in hooks/lib/
findings.proposed_fix: Clarify: inline or helper, dev decides
findings.rationale: Removed prescriptive helper suggestion, dev picks implementation form

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 7 findings applied: spec technical hints rewritten, ACs strengthened (AC-05b added, AC-06 tightened), development_approach strategy updated with codex-corrected tokenizer choice and write-action matching.

codex_consult_iter2.feedback_summary: 10 in-scope PROPOSED_FIX findings + 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY. Caller filtered per codex skill Rule 3. All 10 in-scope findings applied to ticket + context + acceptance-criteria; OBSERVATION_ONLY logged but not actioned (out of stated blocker scope).

findings.issue: shlex.shlex(..., commenters='') is invalid Python kwarg syntax
findings.proposed_fix: Use attribute assignment: lex.commenters = ''; lex.whitespace_split = True
findings.rationale: Critical: dev would otherwise write code that fails to construct

findings.issue: OBJ-6 sweep must cover ticket:74, 92, 99, 124, 169 + context:36, 204, 241
findings.proposed_fix: Update all 7 mentions to the canonical recipe or qualify as 'rejected approach'

findings.issue: AC-01 negative case wording assumes --spec-not-passed; commands/dev.md may auto-detect

findings.rationale: Out of stated blocker scope; BA emits absent/null/string only

findings.issue: AC-07 must include || separator (in existing Layer 1.F contract)
findings.proposed_fix: Add unspaced || case to AC-07 matrix
findings.rationale: Layer 1.F already enumerates || in compound list

findings.issue: Add active command-sub DENY pair $(cmd) and "$(cmd)"
findings.proposed_fix: Add AC-04 cases (g)(h) — unquoted and double-quoted $() must both DENY
findings.rationale: Double-quote does NOT inhibit $() expansion in bash; critical to surface

findings.issue: Don't add quoted backtick/quoted <<< ALLOW (shlex tokenizes identically)
findings.proposed_fix: Omit from must-have ALLOW set; document as SHOULD_HAVE caveat
findings.rationale: Distinguishing quoted vs unquoted backtick requires quote-state tracking outside shlex scope

findings.issue: Add unquoted DENY for <(cmd), >(cmd) and quoted '<(cmd)' ALLOW
findings.rationale: Process-sub forms retain </>/$ prefix on token, distinguishable via prefix check; here-doc skipped (single-line payload limitation)

findings.issue: AC-03 too narrow — only $'-fpr''int' tested; allows special-case fix
findings.proposed_fix: Add $'-de''lete' case to enforce general ANSI-C handler

findings.issue: If AC-05 includes [!abc], update M4 'glob char set' wording
findings.proposed_fix: Broaden M4 from *?[] to POSIX shell-glob bracket expressions
findings.rationale: ! is bracket-expression negation syntax, not a 5th char

findings.issue: AC-08 reliable, but helper must exhaust list(lex) to trigger ValueError
findings.proposed_fix: Add implementation hint to AC-08 — construction alone does not raise
findings.rationale: Without exhaustion, the try/except is a no-op

codex_consult_iter2.feedback_incorporated: All 10 in-scope findings applied to ticket (Reference Source canonical recipe, AC-01 through AC-08 rewritten, M3/M4 wording updated, Technical Hints expanded), context (constraints + strategy + novelty_check rationale rewritten), and acceptance-criteria-20260526-053746.json (8 ACs reissued as parameterized fixture matrices).
